Ana Müller — folder mining summary (synthetic fixture)

Ana's working directory shows three substantive bodies of project work plus
a handful of coursework artifacts. Documented here for the interview-coach
sub-agent so it can ground question answers in actual evidence rather than
generic claims.

PROJECT: MintPay revenue attribution (mintpay-attribution/)

A two-year project from Ana's product-analyst role. The repo contains 38
dbt models organized into staging / intermediate / marts layers, a docs/
directory with the data-contract spec she negotiated with the marketing,
billing, and product engineering teams, and a runbooks/ directory with
the on-call playbook for the daily refresh job. Notable artifacts:
- docs/data-contracts.md — the three signed contracts (one per upstream
  team). Documents what each team commits to deliver, the SLA, and the
  escalation path. Ana wrote this and walked it through three rounds
  of stakeholder review before sign-off.
- runbooks/late-data.md — the protocol for handling late-arriving billing
  events. Two paragraphs, three decisions, one decision tree. Used three
  times in production by other analysts after Ana left.
- analysis/legacy-spreadsheet-deprecation.md — Ana's writeup of the
  rollout plan that retired the 2,000-row attribution spreadsheet. The
  exec team had relied on the spreadsheet for three years; replacing it
  required a parallel-run period and a sign-off from the CFO.

PROJECT: CEU capstone — SaaS retention segmentation (capstone-retention/)

In-progress capstone project, started January 2026, due May 2026. The
candidate is partnering with a Vienna-based SaaS company (Loyalo) on a
real production dataset of 250k users. Notebooks/ has the EDA and feature
engineering; src/ has the production-shape pipeline. Notable:
- src/features/engagement_decay.py — a custom feature Ana derived from
  the literature (modified RFM with logarithmic recency decay) that
  outperformed the off-the-shelf RFM in cross-validation.
- experiments/holdout-design.md — the 2-week holdout test design she
  proposed and Loyalo agreed to run. The treated cohort showed an 18%
  engagement lift; the holdout was statistically significant at p<0.01.
- presentations/loyalo-week6.pdf — an interim presentation to Loyalo's
  CRO. 12 slides, focused on the business case for the segmentation
  rather than the model internals.

PROJECT: Vienna rent regressor (vienna-rent/)

A side project. Python scrapes immobilienscout24 listings, geocodes them,
joins to OEBB transit-time data, and predicts a fair rent. ±€80/month
accuracy on a held-out 2026 sample. Ana used this herself to evaluate
her current apartment.

COURSEWORK: experimentation/ (course: Causal Inference for Business)

Five A/B-test problem sets. Ana scored top of her cohort on the difference-
in-differences problem set. Her writeup of the synthetic-control assignment
(parallel-trends assumption violations) is referenced by the course TA in
office hours as the canonical solution.

OBSERVATIONS

Ana's work consistently shows three strengths interviewers should probe:
(1) She owns the stakeholder negotiation, not just the model — the data
contracts and the spreadsheet deprecation are both organizational artifacts
as much as technical ones. (2) She tests her own work in production —
the 2-week holdout on Loyalo is unusual rigor for a capstone. (3) She
reads the literature and adapts it — the engagement-decay feature is
derivative work but is genuinely improved over the textbook version.

The weakest area in evidence: deep-learning. She has light PyTorch on her
resume but no project artifact demonstrates production-scale neural network
work. If the JD calls for that, push hard on the gap.
